modules.backend.view package#

Submodules#

modules.backend.view.field_view module#

class FieldView[source]#

Bases: object

affineAlign()[source]#

Modify the linear transformation using points from the selected trace.

calibrateMag(trace_lengths: dict, log_event=True)[source]#

Calibrate the pixel mag based on the lengths of given traces.

Params:

trace_lengths (dict): the lengths of the selected traces (name: length)

changeAlignment(new_alignment: str, refresh_data=True)[source]#

Change the alignment setting for the series.

Params:

new_alignment (str): the name of the new alignment

changeBrightness(change)[source]#
changeContrast(change)[source]#
changeSection(new_section_num: int)[source]#

Change the displayed section.

Params:

new_section_num (int): the new section number to display

changeTform(new_tform)[source]#
clearStates()[source]#

Create/clear the states for each section.

copy()[source]#
createFieldView(series: Series)[source]#

Create the field view object.

Params:

series (Series): the series object

createZarrLayer()[source]#

Create a zarr layer.

cut()[source]#
cutTrace(scalpel_trace)[source]#
deleteTraces(traces=None)[source]#
deselectAllTraces()[source]#
findContour(contour_name: str)[source]#

Focus the window view on a given trace.

Params:

contour_name (str): the name of the contour to focus on

findFlag(flag: Flag)[source]#

Find a flag on the current section

findTrace(trace_name: str, index=0)[source]#

Focus the window view on a given trace.

Params:

trace_name (str): the name of the trace to focus on index (int): find the nth trace on the section

generateView(pixmap_dim: tuple, generate_image=True, generate_traces=True, blend=False)[source]#

Generate the view seen by the user in the main window.

Params:

pixmap_dim (tuple): the w and h of the pixmap view generate_image (bool): whether or not to redraw the image generate_traces (bool): whether or not to redraw the traces

hideTraces(traces=None, hide=True)[source]#
home()[source]#

Set the view to the image.

makeNegative(negative=True)[source]#
mergeSelectedTraces(traces: list = None, merge_attrs=False)[source]#
moveTo(snum: int, x: float, y: float)[source]#

Move to a specified section number and coordinates (used from 3D scene).

Params:

snum (int): the section number to move to x (int): the x coordinate to focus on y (int): the y coordinate to focus on

newTrace(pix_trace, tracing_trace, closed=True, log_event=True)[source]#
paste()[source]#
pasteAttributes()[source]#
placeFlag(title, pix_x, pix_y, color, comment)[source]#
placeGrid(pix_x, pix_y, trace, w, h, dx, dy, nx, ny)[source]#
placeStamp(pix_x, pix_y, stamp)[source]#
propagateTo(to_end: bool = True, log_event=True)[source]#

Propagate the stored transform to the start/end of series.

Params:

to_end (bool): True propagates to the end, False propagates to beginning

quickAlign()[source]#

Do a quick translational alignment of the current secion and b section.

reload(clear_states=False)[source]#

Reload the section data (used if section files were modified, usually through object list).

reloadImage()[source]#

Reload the section images (used if transform or image source is modified).

resizeWindow(pixmap_dim: tuple)[source]#

Convert the window to match the proportions of the pixmap.

Params:

pixmap_dim (tuple): the w and h of the pixmap view

rotateTform(cc=True)[source]#

Rotate the section transform.

saveState()[source]#

Save the current traces and transform.

ALSO updates the lists.

selectAllTraces()[source]#
selectFlag(flag: Flag)[source]#

Select/deselect a single flag.

Params:

flag (Flag): the flag to select

selectTrace(trace: Trace)[source]#

Select/deselect a single trace.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to select

selectTraces(traces: list[Trace], ztraces_i: list)[source]#

Select/deselect a set of traces.

Params:

traces (list[Trace]): the traces to select

selectZtrace(ztrace_i: tuple)[source]#

Select/deselect a single ztrace point.

Params:

ztrace_i (tuple): the ztrace, index of point selected

seriesUndo(redo=False)[source]#

Undo an action across the series.

Params:

redo (bool): True if should redo instead of undo

setPropagationMode(propagate: bool)[source]#

Set the propagation mode.

Params:

propagate (bool): whether to begin or finish propagating

setView(mag: float)[source]#

Set the scaling value for the view.

Params:

scaling (float): the new scaling value

swapABsections()[source]#

Switch the A and B sections.

toggleHideAllTraces()[source]#

Hide the trace layer.

toggleHideImage()[source]#

Toggle hide the image from view.

toggleShowAllTraces()[source]#

Toggle show all traces regardless of hiding status.

translate(dx: float, dy: float)[source]#

Translate the transform OR the selected traces.

Params:

dx (float): x-translate dy (float): y-translate

translateTform(dx: float, dy: float)[source]#

Translate the transform for the entire section. Params:

dx (float): x-translate dy (float): y-translate

undoState(redo=False)[source]#

Undo last action (switch to last state).

unhideAllTraces()[source]#
updateData(clear_tracking=True)[source]#

Update the series data object and the tables.

modules.backend.view.image_layer module#

class ImageLayer(section: Section, series: Series)[source]#

Bases: object

changeBrightness(change: int, log_event=True)[source]#

Change the brightness of the section.

Params:

change (int): the degree to which brightness is changed

changeContrast(change: int, log_event=True)[source]#

Change the contrast of the section.

Params:

change (float): the degree to which contrast is changed

generateImageArray(pixmap_dim: tuple, window: list)[source]#

Generate the image layer.

Params:

pixmap_dim (tuple): the w and h of the 2D array window (list): the x, y, w, and h of the field window

Returns:

(numpy.ndarray) the image as a numpy array

generateImageLayer(pixmap_dim: tuple, window: list) → QPixmap[source]#

Generate the image layer.

Params:

pixmap_dim (tuple): the w and h of the main window window (list): the x, y, w, and h of the field window

Returns:

image_layer (QPixmap): the image laye

loadImage()[source]#

Load the image.

setBrightness(b: int, log_event=True)[source]#

Set the brightness of the section.

setContrast(c: int, log_event=True)[source]#

Set the contrast of the section.

setSrcDir(src_dir: str)[source]#

Set the immediate source directory and reload image.

Params:

src_dir (str): the new directory for the images

getBoundingRect(points: list)[source]#

Get the bounding rectangle and shift in origin for a set of points.

Params:

points (list): a list of points

Returns:

(tuple): xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax

modules.backend.view.section_layer module#

class SectionLayer(section: Section, series: Series, load_image_layer=True)[source]#

Bases: ImageLayer, TraceLayer

changeTform(new_tform: Transform, log_event=True)[source]#

Set the transform for the image.

Params:

new_tform (Transform): the new transform to set for the section

generateView(pixmap_dim: tuple, window: list, generate_image=True, generate_traces=True, hide_traces=False, show_all_traces=False, hide_image=False)[source]#

Generate the pixmap view for the section.

Params:

pixmap_dim (tuple): the dimensions of the view window (list): the x, y, w, h of the field view generate_image (bool): whether or not to regenerate the image generate_traces (bool): whether or not to regenerate the traces

modules.backend.view.trace_layer module#

class TraceLayer(section: Section, series: Series)[source]#

Bases: object

cutTrace(knife_trace: list, log_event=True)[source]#

Cuts the selected trace along the knife line.

Params:

knife_trace (list): the knife trace in pixmap points

eraseArea(pix_x: int, pix_y: int)[source]#

Erase an area of the field.

Params:

pix_x (int): the x coord for erasing pix_y (int): the y coord for erasing

generateLabelsArray(pixmap_dim: tuple, window: list, traces: list[Trace], tform: Transform = None)[source]#

Generate numpy array with traces drawn as labels.

Params:

pixmap_dim (tuple): the w and h of the 2D array window (list): the view of the window (x, y, w, h) traces (list[Traces]): the traces to include as labels tform (Transform): the unique transform to apply to the traces

Returns:

(np.ndarray): the numpy array with traces drawn in as labels

generateTraceLayer(pixmap_dim: tuple, window: list, show_all_traces=False, window_moved=True) → QPixmap[source]#

Generate the traces on a transparent background.

Params:

pixmap_dim (tuple): the w and h of the pixmap to be output window (list): the view of the window (x, y, w, h) show_all_traces (bool): True if all traces are displayed regardless of hidden status window_moved (bool): True if the window has moved (upstream: same as generate_image)

Returns:

(QPixmap): the pixmap with traces drawn in

getCopiedTraces(cut=False) → list[source]#

Called when user presses Ctrl+C or Ctrl+X.

Params:

cut (bool): whether or not to delete the traces

Returns:

(list): the traces to copy

getTrace(pix_x: float, pix_y: float) → Trace[source]#

“Return the closest trace to the given field coordinates.

Params:

pix_x (float): the x-coord of the point in the widget pix_y (float): the y-coord of the point in the widget

Returns:

(Trace): the closest trace

getTraces(pix_poly: list) → list[Trace][source]#

“Select all traces that are completely in a polygon

Params:

pix_poly (list): a list of screen points

Returns:

(list[Trace]): the list of traces within the polygon

getZsegment(pix_x: float, pix_y: float, radius=10)[source]#

Find the closest ztrace segment (does not have a point on the section).

Params:

pix_x (float): the screen pixel x pix_y (float): the screen pixel y radius (float): the max radius for finding a ztrace segment

mergeSelectedTraces(traces: list = None, merge_attrs=False, log_event=True)[source]#

Merge all selected traces.

Params:

merge_attrs (bool): True if only trace attributes should be merged

newTrace(pix_trace: list, base_trace: Trace, closed=True, reduce_points=True, log_event=True)[source]#

Create a new trace from pixel coordinates.

Params:

pix_trace (list): pixel coordinates for the new trace base_trace (Trace): the trace containing the desired attributes closed (bool): whether or not the new trace is closed

pasteAttributes(traces: list[Trace])[source]#

Called when the user pressed Ctrl+B.

pasteTraces(traces: list[Trace])[source]#

Called when the user presses Ctrl+V.

Params:

traces (list): a list of trace objects to paste

placeFlag(title: str, pix_x: int, pix_y: int, color: tuple, comment: str)[source]#

Create a flag on the section.

Params:

title (str): the title of the flag pix_x (float): the x-coord of the mouse location pix_y (float): the y-coord of the mouse location color (tuple): the color of the flag comment (str): the flag comment

placeGrid(pix_x: float, pix_y: float, ref_trace: Trace, w: float, h: float, dx: float, dy: float, nx: int, ny: int)[source]#

Place a grid on the field.

Params:

pix_x (float): the x-coord of the mouse location pix_y (float): the y-coord of the mouse location trace (Trace): the trace to use in the grid w (float): the desired width of the trace h (float): the desired height of the trace dx (float): the x distance between traces in the grid dy (float): the y distance between traces in the grid nx (int): the number of columns ny (int): the number of rows

placeStamp(pix_x: int, pix_y: int, trace: Trace)[source]#

Called when mouse is pressed in stamp mode.

Creates a stamp centered on the mouse location.

Params:

pix_x (int): pixel x-coord to place stamp pix_y (int): pixel y-coord to place stamp trace (Trace): the trace to place down

pointToPix(pt: tuple, apply_tform=True, tform: Transform = None, qpoint=False) → tuple[source]#

Return the pixel point corresponding to a field point.

Params:

pt (tuple): the trace to convert apply_tform (bool): true if section transform should be applied to the point tform (Transform): the transform to apply (otherwise, uses series data) qpoints (bool): True if points should be converted QPoint

Returns:

(tuple): the pixel point

traceToPix(trace: Trace, tform: Transform = None, qpoints=False) → list[source]#

Return the set of pixel points corresponding to a trace.

Params:

trace (Trace): the trace to convert tform (Transform): the transform to apply qpoints (bool): True if points should be converted QPoint

Returns:

(list): list of pixel points

boundsOverlap(b1: tuple, b2: tuple)[source]#

Check if two bounding boxes intersect.

Params:

b1 (tuple): xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax b2 (tuple): xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax

Returns:

(bool): True if bounds have any overlap

drawArrow(painter: QPainter, line: QLine, p1_arrow=True, p2_arrow=True)[source]#

Draw an line with one or two arrowheads.

Params:

painter (QPainter): the painter used to draw the arrow line (QLine): the ztrace qline p1_arrow (bool): True if arrowhead on the first point p2_arrow (bool): True if arrowhead on the second point

drawArrowHead(painter: QPainter, pt: QPoint, theta: float, l=5)[source]#

Draw an arrowhead in a given direction.

Params:

painter (QPainter): the painter to draw the arrowhead pt (QPoint): the point on which the arrowhead is located theta (float): the angle (in radians) of the arrowhead (cc from x-axis) l (int): the length of the arrowhead

getTheta(line: QLine)[source]#

Get the angle a line makes with the x-axis.

Params:

line (QLine): the qline to get the angle for

hashName(name: str)[source]#

Create a hash label for a name.

Params:

name (str): the name to hash

modules.backend.view.zarr_layer module#

class ZarrLayer(series: Series)[source]#

Bases: object

deselectAll()[source]#

Deselect all the IDs.

generateZarrLayer(section: Section, pixmap_dim: tuple, window: list) → QPixmap[source]#

Generate the zarr layer.

Params:

section (Section): the current section object pixmap_dim (tuple): the w and h of the main window window (list): the x, y, w, and h of the field window

Returns:

zarr_layer (QPixmap): the zarr layer

getID(pix_x: int, pix_y: int)[source]#

Get an ID from screen pixel coordinates.

Params:

pix_x (int): the x coord in screen pixels pix_y (int): the y coord in screen pixels

loadZarrData()[source]#

Load the relevant data from the zarr file.

mergeLabels()[source]#

Merge the selected labels.

selectID(pix_x: int, pix_y: int)[source]#

Select the ID at a given screen coord.

Params:

pix_x (int): the x coord in screen pixels pix_y (int): the y coord in screen pixels
